info.name: Shipping Line Carrier APIs
info.description:
Direct tracking APIs for COSCO, Hapag-Lloyd, ONE Line, MSC and HMM.

HMM: the tracking call needs a per-session CSRF token. A pre-request script on the HMM request fetches that token from the tracking page and sets it as the x-csrf-token header automatically — just press Send.

query.description: Cache-buster; any current epoch-millis value works.

request.description: Public COSCO tracking API. IMPORTANT: the path segment is the BARE bill number WITHOUT the 'COSU' prefix (e.g. 6452489450, not COSU6452489450). With the prefix the API returns 200 + 'No data'. Returns trackingPath (POL/POD), cargoTrackingContainer (containers), and actualShipment (per-leg vessel schedule).

Hapag-Lloyd internal tracking API. The 'x-token: public' header is required — no login or Cloudflare challenge. Returns groups[].events[] with eventDescription, eventLocation, eventDate/Time, eventTransport, eventVoyageNo, and eventClassifierCode (Actual vs Planned).

item.name: ONE Line - Track and trace search

request.description: ONE Line internal track-and-trace API (no login). search_type is BKG_NO for a booking number or BL_NO for a bill of lading; put the reference in search_text (the ONEY prefix may be stripped). Returns data[] of per-container items with por/pod, vesselVoyage, and cargoEvents.

MSC tracking API. Notes: trackingMode MUST be an integer (0), and the 'X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest' header is required (without it the API returns 200 with an empty body). MSC's Akamai edge may return 403 in Postman because it checks the TLS fingerprint of a real browser; the app gets past this with a Chrome-impersonating HTTP client.

exec: // HMM requires a CSRF token from its tracking page (tied to the session).
// Fetch the page first, extract the _csrf token, and save it for the POST.
if (err) { console.log('CSRF fetch failed:', err); return; }
if (m) { pm.collectionVariables.set('hmm_csrf', m[1]); console.log('CSRF token set:', m[1]); }
else { console.log('CSRF token not found on page'); }

request.description: HMM tracking endpoint. Two caveats for a standalone call: (1) it expects a CSRF token (x-csrf-token header) obtained by first GET-ing the TrackNTrace.do page and reading the _csrf meta tag; (2) the app sends this over HTTP/1.1 with a Chrome-impersonating client. Without the token the request may not return data. Documented here as the real endpoint.
